reasoning: As of November 9, 2025, Bitcoin is trading around $110,000, having largely consolidated sideways since March 2025. With only 52 days remaining in the year, Bitcoin needs a roughly 36% jump to hit the $150,000 threshold. Using a standard first-passage probability calculation for an asset with 50-60% annualized volatility, the mathematical odds of touching a +36% barrier in under two months is approximately 10-17%. While Q4 is historically a strong period for crypto breakouts and institutional adoption remains robust, macroeconomic uncertainties (such as US-China tariff tensions) and months of flat consolidation indicate that a sudden parabolic surge to $150K before the year closes is relatively unlikely. Therefore, I place the probability of it making this exact 36% upside run before December 31 at 13%.
